window_summary: This window tracks the recent v2ray/v2ray-core release stream from v4.14.0 through v4.31.0, covering protocol enhancements, security fixes, and new features such as VMessAEAD, VLESS, and XTLS.

chunks.title: v4.21.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.21.0
Tag: v4.21.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2019-10-24T16:21:48Z; created_at: 2019-10-24T14:46:10Z
Release notes:
## Feature
* HTTP Outbound supported #1813 @flyxl
* Email shown in access log #1800 #1767 (only Vmess and Shadowsocks) @Gondnat @vcptr
* API add `StatsService.GetSysStats` #1794 @vcptr
* Go 1.13+ required, for now, 1.13.3 @vcptr
## Fix
* transparent proxy mode  UDP performance #1940 @enihcam
* API config causes crash #1810 @winguse
* Shadowsocks bug #1850 @yuanmomo
* Security loophole in Websocket #1950 #1955 @keepalivesrc @vcptr
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.21.0, the release note adds HTTP outbound support, email in access log for VMess and Shadowsocks, a StatsService.GetSysStats API, and requires Go 1.13+. It fixes transparent proxy UDP performance, API config crash, Shadowsocks bug, and a security loophole in WebSocket.

chunks.title: v4.22.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.22.0
Tag: v4.22.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2019-12-29T09:39:14Z; created_at: 2019-12-29T09:18:34Z
Release notes:
### Feature
* DOH DNS [WIKI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DNS_over_HTTPS) @vcptr
* ExpectIPs in DNS  https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/pull/2037 @weaving118
* Support external domain file for dns hosts https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/6bfe044cfa55aba44c23bd81db6687490a757bab @forever8938
### Fix
* ARM Multiple versions https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/78d940ddac04cc62a09cba09932dfe1aa134e3f1 https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/9d1cac168091d4484b8d05414b77b6e491cf7955
*   KCPLarge buffer size too small   https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/2a78af34fdc9a59dc245d1567798d3e9d3167905
*  session key unexported https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/afd805c7ba92aa039e0cdd1affb516149779b740
*  properly propagate Reader errors  https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/8da0a538454ef30e39ab8bf4077a23dcebb4b6e1
*  fix ReadBuffer() https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/commit/f7abe353df088e958949f7dd2b405671dcff80bc
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.22.0, the release note adds DNS over HTTPS (DOH), ExpectIPs in DNS, and support for external domain file for DNS hosts. It fixes ARM multiple versions, KCP large buffer size, session key unexported, properly propagate Reader errors, and ReadBuffer().

chunks.title: V4.23.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: V4.23.0
Tag: v4.23.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-03-22T12:24:50Z; created_at: 2020-03-19T10:51:58Z
Release notes:
### Feature
* MultiConfig (-config base.json -c dns.json -c route.json etc.. ) [WIKI](https://www.v2fly.org/chapter_02/multiple_config.html) @vcptr
* FreeBSD/PF Support #2246  @lucifer9
### Fix
* DNS optimization #2212  @mzz2017
* Install Script Optimization @xymopen
* Add SO_REUSEPORT to inbound fix https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/1881
* Systemd Enhancement @unknowndev233
* Cert Command Parameter Typo @ToutyRater
* Fix tproxy udp "unable to get destination" in IPv6 environment  @lucifer9
*  Doh Route Strategy Optimized #2305  @vcptr
* Update GEO(Site, IP) Data @Loyalsoldier
* Build Script Enhancement @IceCodeNew
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.23.0, the release note adds MultiConfig support, FreeBSD/PF support, and fixes DNS optimization, install script, SO_REUSEPORT, systemd enhancement, cert command typo, tproxy UDP in IPv6, Doh route strategy, updates GEO data, and enhances build script.

chunks.title: v4.23.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.23.3
Tag: v4.23.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-01T15:15:52Z; created_at: 2020-06-01T15:14:49Z
Release notes:
### Fix ####
* Issued an emergency fix for VMess weakness described in v2ray#2523
This fix can significantly hindrance attack based on the weakness described. Only servers need to be updated to apply this emergency fix, it not expected for well-behaved clients to be influenced. A more permanent solution is underway.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.23.3, the release note issues an emergency fix for a VMess weakness described in v2ray#2523, significantly hindering attacks based on that weakness. Only servers need to be updated.

chunks.title: v4.23.4
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.23.4
Tag: v4.23.4; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-03T10:08:00Z; created_at: 2020-06-03T10:03:50Z
Release notes:
### Fix ###
* Issued further fixs for VMess weakness described in v2ray#2523 https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2539
* Issued a fix for Fake HTTP Header weakness described in https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2537
### Breaking Changes ###
* For VMess connections, clients cannot rely on servers to indicate issues in connectivity or password mismatch by closing connection. Well behaved clients are not influenced.
* For VMess servers, it is not recommended to change UUID frequently as VMess will procedurally generate its connection drain and close pattern based on UUID it has when the first connection arrives. This is very unlikely to influence users that are using V2Ray as a standalone software. However, for those dynamically add and remove UUID, it is recommended to connect to V2Ray's port immediately after starting it before adding more UUIDs with API and make sure there is at least one UUID in the configure file even if you plan to add more later.
* For users using Fake HTTP Header, it is necessary to keep clients and servers synchronized on fake HTTP header settings, and assign a path with sufficient entropy. From now on, if the request path is not expected, the server will abort the connection.
* Server will by default wait 60 seconds for the handshakes to complete before closing the connection instead of 4 seconds. This may increase server load if the server is under a DDoS attack. This allows the server to blend into normal TCP servers better and its benefit is significantly higher than potential downsides. This behavior is tweakable with the configure file.
### Security Advisory ###
* From now on, the VMess protocol will drain any unrecognized or invalid connection based on procedurally generate connection drain and close patterns. It can neutralize all known "replay and observe close" attacks. The base drain size and drain size jitter will be generated based on UUID a VMess inbound have when the first connection arrives. For each server, these values will be different and remain constant for that server. The attacker will not be able to aggregate results in order to identify server identity. If you have multiple VMess inbounds on the same host, the protection offered by procedurally generated connection drain and close patterns will be slightly encumbered.
* Fake HTTP Header will from now on imitate standard HTTP server behavior better and is able to significantly hindrance active probing. However, it is only designed to circumvent ISP's QoS, rate limiting, throttling, and sabotage of unrecognized connection. For an attacker with privileged access to network path, Fake HTTP Header does not offer adequate protection against specialized detection or manual inspections by attackers with special tools and knowledge.
## A Request for Users and Client Authors ##
Please refer to https://github.com/v2fly/v2ray-core/releases for further updates instead of the V2Ray release page. Currently, update in V2Fly will be mirrored to the V2Ray release page but this will NOT continue indefinitely.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.23.4, the release note issues further fixes for VMess weakness and a fix for Fake HTTP Header weakness. It introduces breaking changes: VMess connections drain unrecognized connections based on UUID, servers should not change UUID frequently, Fake HTTP Header requires synchronized settings and sufficient entropy, and server handshake wait time increases to 60 seconds. A security advisory details procedural connection drain and close patterns for VMess and improved HTTP imitation for Fake HTTP Header.

chunks.title: v4.24.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.24.2
Tag: v4.24.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-10T15:10:55Z; created_at: 2020-06-10T14:12:38Z
Release notes:
### New Functions ###
* VMess's new header format experiment begins. You can now enable VMessAEAD to be better protected. Documents [EN](https://www.v2fly.org/en/configuration/protocols/vmess.html) [CH](https://www.v2fly.org/chapter_02/protocols/vmess.html) are updated.  See extra-VMessAEADdoc.zip for a more detailed explanation available in both Chinese and English.
* mKCP can now be encrypted to resist recognition, address issue mentioned in https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2530 https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2253 https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2131. Documents [EN](https://www.v2fly.org/en/configuration/transport/mkcp.html) [CH](https://www.v2fly.org/chapter_02/transport/mkcp.html) are updated.
### Breaking Changes ###
* To slow down certain replay attacks, taint mechanic for VMess MD5 authentication data is introduced. Unless the server is under attack, a well-behaved client is not influenced. Pay attention to this change if you are designing a "V2Ray Panel",  "Airport" or "V2Ray Service Provider". Documents [EN](https://www.v2fly.org/en/configuration/protocols/vmess.html) [CH](https://www.v2fly.org/chapter_02/protocols/vmess.html) are updated.
Minor fixes from https://github.com/v2fly/v2ray-core/pull/12  https://github.com/v2fly/v2ray-core/pull/10 included.
* Updated Golang tool-chain. Thanks @rprx
* LocalAddr() in UDP workers will now return correct local addr. Thanks @zhuobixin
## About AV warnings ##
We are aware some anti-virus software detects V2Ray as malware, which it isn't,  but we can't afford to pay the fee needed to make them not detect V2Ray as malware.  Microsoft's AV detects V2Ray as "Trojan:Win32/Wacatac.C!ml" , "Program:Win32/Wacapew.C!ml" each time we release a new version, and I have to manually contact them each time to remove it from the malware database. If you have more information to report please leave them [here](https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2546). These AVs are known to generate false-positive results: "Microsoft", "McAfee", "Cylance", "Symantec", "F-Secure", "SecureAge APEX", "Cybereason", "Cynet", "MaxSecure", "F-Secure", "Avira", "AVAST", "AVG", "Rising"(瑞星).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.24.2, the release note begins VMessAEAD header format experiment, adds mKCP encryption, introduces taint mechanic for VMess MD5 authentication data, updates Golang tool-chain, fixes LocalAddr() in UDP workers, and notes AV false positives.

chunks.title: v4.25.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.25.0
Tag: v4.25.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-19T15:49:07Z; created_at: 2020-06-19T15:10:25Z
Release notes:
### Fix ###
* Further remove the identity leakage with TLS ClientHello from https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/pull/2521 thank @darhwa
* Fix UDP stability issue in Socks5 inbound, Shadowsocks inbound, and Dokodemo( TProxy ) inbound. This should alleviate unnecessary CPU, memory usage, and premature disconnection issue typically triggered by playing games, video conference, and Torrenting. See https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2565
*thank @JimHan75d8c5 @xiaokangwang and Other testers @1265578519 @zhj9709 @Kylejustknows etc*
* Added ARM v5 binary in the release. This should allow legacy ARM devices to run V2Ray correctly. See https://github.com/v2ray/v2ray-core/issues/2536
### Breaking Changes ###
* The file structure for ARM release files has changed.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.25.0, the release note further removes identity leakage with TLS ClientHello, fixes UDP stability in Socks5, Shadowsocks, and Dokodemo inbounds, adds ARM v5 binary, and changes ARM release file structure.

chunks.title: v4.25.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.25.1
Tag: v4.25.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-06-23T23:25:25Z; created_at: 2020-06-23T14:23:22Z

## Fix
Improve ARM platform stability. Thank @gitgayhub @rprx and other testers
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.25.1, the release note improves ARM platform stability.

chunks.title: v4.26.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.26.0
Tag: v4.26.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-07-08T08:26:52Z; created_at: 2020-07-07T13:16:29Z
Release notes:
Updates
* V2Fly’s signing infrastructure has been revised. You can from now on verify V2Fly’s release with the “Release” signed manifest file with v2ctl.
* VMessAEAD has been updated to rely more on AEAD.
* Outbound traffic accounting API. Thanks @yuhan6665
* HTTP outbound can now handle HTTP2 over TLS, HTTP 1.1 over TLS in addition to HTTP 1.1 proxy traffic. Thanks @darhwa
* ARM release will from now on have an explicit version in it. Thanks @rprx
* V2Ray will from now on generate Windows 32 ARM binary release.
* Minor code tidy up. Thanks Roger Shimizu.
* Minor code tidy up. Thanks @agioi
* Fix crash when the client supplied an invalid domain name to DoH. Thanks @DuckSoft
Breaking Change
* VMessAEAD has been updated and needs both client and server to be the same version for it to work.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.26.0, the release note revises signing infrastructure, updates VMessAEAD to rely more on AEAD, adds outbound traffic accounting API, HTTP outbound handles HTTP2/1.1 over TLS, ARM release has explicit version, generates Windows 32 ARM binary, minor code tidy ups, and fixes DoH crash on invalid domain. Breaking change: VMessAEAD requires same client/server version.

chunks.title: v4.27.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.27.0
Tag: v4.27.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-08-06T12:10:11Z; created_at: 2020-08-06T11:51:58Z
Release notes:
### Feature
* VLESS PREVIEW 1.3  https://www.v2fly.org/config/protocols/vless.html
* Add Linux riscv64 release @rprx
### Fix
* Shadowsocks supports cipher as None  @yuhan6665
* Add access log for Dokodemo inbound  @lucifer9
* Some typo fixes @DuckSoft
* Fix the error of missing curl in Dockerfile @kallydev
* GitHub Actions push to docker hub  @kallydev
* Optimize SO_REUSEPORT implementation @Vigilans
* DOH supports HTTP/2 @darhwa
* Apply Sockopt from inbound config to Dokodemo Tproxy response connection @Vigilans
### Breaking Change
* VMessAEAD has been updated and needs both client and server to be the same version for it to work.
* Release file structure changes.
### ⚠️ VMess AEAD will be used when `alterId` is `0` in V2Ray v4.28.0+
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.27.0, the release note adds VLESS PREVIEW 1.3, Linux riscv64 release, Shadowsocks None cipher, access log for Dokodemo, typo fixes, Dockerfile curl fix, GitHub Actions push to Docker Hub, SO_REUSEPORT optimization, DOH HTTP/2 support, and Sockopt for Dokodemo Tproxy. Breaking changes: VMessAEAD update, release file structure changes, and VMess AEAD will be used when alterId is 0 in v4.28.0+.

## Features
Parse X-Forwarded-For in http transport @lucifer9
## Fix
Fix according to staticcheck result @Loyalsoldier
Fix: Bound check when accessing DNS server's ipIndexMap @Vigilans
Refine code @Loyalsoldier
### ⚠️ VMess AEAD will be used when `alterId` is `0` in V2Ray v4.28.0+
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.27.5, the release note adds parsing of X-Forwarded-For in HTTP transport, fixes according to staticcheck, fixes bound check in DNS server's ipIndexMap, and refines code. It also notes that VMess AEAD will be used when alterId is 0 in v4.28.0+.

## Fixes

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.28.2, the release note fixes Routing Context GetUser() and uses string for Attributes Value, fixes coarse-grained mutex in HTTP outbound, supports disabling VMessAead by OS environment, and refines workflows.

chunks.title: v4.31.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: v2ray/v2ray-core
Release: v4.31.0
Tag: v4.31.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-10-10T16:50:42Z; created_at: 2020-10-09T04:27:55Z
Release notes:
## Warning
* Remove Shadowsosks OTA
* Compiling v2ray-core requires Go 1.15+
## Fixes
* Fix Trojan inbound access log
## Features
* VLESS PREVIEW 2.4
* Add XTLS Direct Mode
https://www.v2fly.org/config/protocols/vless.html#xtls-%E9%BB%91%E7%A7%91%E6%8A%80
* Add XTLS support to mKCP
* Add full VLESS fallbacks support to Trojan
* Add padding to abstract UNIX domain socket in fallbacks
* Allow startup when VLESS inbound clients are zero
* Update to IETF QUIC draft-29
## Refactor
* VLESS fallbacks & XTLS other improvements
* Stats: Implements blocking/non-blocking messaging of Channel. @Vigilans
## Chores
* Refine Azure Pipelines & Bazel: remove unnecessary steps. @Loyalsoldier
* Refine code *26. @Loyalsoldier
## Build and compiling
* Compiler uses `gccgo`, remove flags `gcflags` and `asmflags`
* Make builds reproducible
## Notice
The project gets rid of `GOPATH` mode entirely from now on. Use `Git` and `go mod` command as your first choice while developing.
For more information, visit:
* https://www.v2fly.org/developer/intro/compile.html
* https://www.v2fly.org/developer/intro/guide.html
# Download: https://github.com/v2fly/v2ray-core/releases/tag/v4.31.0
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release v4.31.0, the release note removes Shadowsocks OTA, requires Go 1.15+, fixes Trojan inbound access log, adds VLESS PREVIEW 2.4, XTLS Direct Mode, XTLS support to mKCP, full VLESS fallbacks support to Trojan, padding to abstract UNIX domain socket in fallbacks, allows startup with zero VLESS inbound clients, updates to IETF QUIC draft-29, refactors VLESS fallbacks and XTLS, implements blocking/non-blocking messaging of Channel in Stats, refines Azure Pipelines and Bazel, refines code, uses gccgo compiler, makes builds reproducible, and gets rid of GOPATH mode.

questions.query_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core releases, which release adds DNS over HTTPS (DOH) and ExpectIPs in DNS?

answer_support.answer_text: adds DNS over HTTPS (DOH), ExpectIPs in DNS

questions.query_text: Across the v2ray/v2ray-core releases, how does the VMess security fix in v4.23.3 differ from the VMessAEAD experiment introduced in v4.24.2?

options.text: v4.23.3 issues an emergency fix for a VMess weakness requiring only server update, while v4.24.2 begins a VMessAEAD header format experiment and introduces a taint mechanic for MD5 authentication data.
v4.23.3 adds VMessAEAD support, while v4.24.2 removes VMess entirely.
Both releases only update TLS cipher suites without VMess changes.
v4.23.3 introduces mKCP encryption, while v4.24.2 fixes a Windows ReadV issue.

questions.expected_answer: v4.23.3 issues an emergency fix for a VMess weakness requiring only server update, while v4.24.2 begins a VMessAEAD header format experiment and introduces a taint mechanic for MD5 authentication data.

answer_support.answer_text: Issued an emergency fix for VMess weakness described in v2ray#2523 ... Only servers need to be updated
VMess's new header format experiment begins. You can now enable VMessAEAD ... taint mechanic for VMess MD5 authentication data is introduced

questions.query_text: In the v2ray/v2ray-core release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds VLESS PREVIEW 1.3 and notes VMess AEAD will be used when alterId is 0 in v4.28.0+, (2) a release that issues an emergency fix for a VMess weakness and only requires server update, (3) a release that begins the VMessAEAD header format experiment and adds mKCP encryption, (4) a release that adds VLESS PREVIEW 2.4 and XTLS Direct Mode.

answer_support.answer_text: Issued an emergency fix for VMess weakness
VMess's new header format experiment begins ... mKCP can now be encrypted
VLESS PREVIEW 1.3 ... VMess AEAD will be used when alterId is 0 in V2Ray v4.28.0+
VLESS PREVIEW 2.4 ... Add XTLS Direct Mode
